title: The Forest

notes: mouse is going to right bottom when click or move with mouse
title: The Forest

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
I'll list the issues I encounter, the solution attempted and it's result.
 
ISSUE | FIX | STATUS
1- Failed to launch a new game | added PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 | Fixed.

2- Initial cutscene broken, broken gameplay | TZ=America/New_York | Fixed.

3- Changing settings has no effect on game | unset LC_ALL && | Fixed.

4- Text in survival book not rendered (black boxes) | Installed fonts with winetricks | Not fixed.

5- Save attempts result in black screen | unset LC_ALL && | Not fixed

All the commands are added as  "launch options" on game properties, in the following order:
"unset LC_ALL && PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 TZ=America/New_York %command%"

Further workarounds will be reported here.
title: The Forest

notes: The fps are a little bit low at medium settings ~ 30 but its playable after all

title: The Forest

notes:
To temporary fix the mouse issue, press Alt + Enter twice ingame, but it is better if you use am gamepad (e.g. Xbox 360 controller). The game doesn't exit correct, you must stop it with Alt + F4 or the the command: killall -KILL TheForest.exe . To save the changes in the ingame options, add the launch option: unset LC_ALL && %command%
Performance is great (over 60fps on middle to high settings on my system, also in fights and caves), multiplayer works too

notes: Mouse issue is fixed with 3.16-8 Beta. Performance still isn't too great. taskset -c 0,2,4,6 %command% helps with ryzen to get a bit better framerates. Doesn't hang on exit either anymore
title: The Forest

notes: Runs out of box, but with crippled performance and using all the CPU cores on Ryzen. 35-45 FPS only. taskset -c 0-7 makes the performance go up to 45-70 FPS. Disabling SMT by running echo 0 > /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/online, with replacing the * with the odd numbers like (1,3,5,7 etc) and starting the game like that will yield 65-110 fps. You can turn the cores back on after the Game start with echo 1 > /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/online. Played Multiplayer and Singleplayer with no issues. There's a odd mouse issue after some alt+tabing but alt+enter twice, usually fixes that.
title: The Forest

notes: Runs without any tweaks. Performance is between 36 and 58 FPS. Not as good as on windows, but still playable without any concerns.
title: The Forest

notes: It worked quite well. No performance issues, but when in-game, the camera moves a little wonky. No problem with Alt-Tab so far.
title: The Forest

notes:
Works out of the box but with bad performance. To fix performance it's necessary to turn off half of the cpu cores before starting the game and re-enable them after the game has started!

Command to turn off cpu cores (run as root): "echo 0 > /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu<core number>/online"
Command to turn core on again: "echo 1 > /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu<core number>/online"
title: The Forest

notes: Now everything works perfectly out of the box. No mouse issue, no crashes on exit and the ingame settings will be saved automaticly

title: The Forest

notes: Runs but with unplayable (ie single-digit) FPS unless using "unset LC_ALL && PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" in the launch options.. performance was still 1/2 of what it is on Windows (running 25-30 fps on high settings, on Windows it's a stable 60 maxed out) but it's at least playable now. The "unset LC_ALL &&" part is required to make settings changes stick. Tried all kinds of other tweaks mentioned here (disabling various cores, older versions of Proton, etc) and nothing else worked.

notes: This runs perfectly out of the box! All I did was install and hit play, configured the video settings accordingly and hopped into a game with a friend. The only issue I had was about 3 hours in, the game froze me in a multiplayer game and I had to kill -9 the process. I started back up and was good to go. Game runs fine and I didn't notice any frame dips. All in all great experience and I look forward to putting in many more hours.

notes: Without launch options the game crashes with an error message in a wine window and generates a crash log. I had to use PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% in the launch options for the game to start. Playable at high graphical settings but runs about 30-40 fps. Low settings give me 50-60 fps. It's playable but nothing fancy. I've tried other suggested tweaks to gain more performance but with no noticeable gain.

title: The Forest

notes: Works out of the box. Disabling SMT for AMD processors gives around double the performance so it is highly recommended (I tested it out after reading another user's report). This also seems to be the case for a wide range of games that run under proton.
title: The Forest

notes: Okay, so I've experimented a bit here and the taskset trick DOES work. I've also found that the forest performs best with 6 threads, even if you have an 8 core cpu. No launch flag 2560x1440p MAXED: ~30 Fps. 8 Threads: ~50-80fps but heavy stutter. 4 Threads: ~40-60Fps and stutter. 6 Threads: ~70-85fps average and really smooth. I disabled steam overlay and used these launch options without the quotes: "DXVK_HUD=1 taskset -c 0,2,4,6 %command%"
title: The Forest

title: The Forest

cpu: AMD A10-7860K Radeon R7, 12 Compute Cores 4C+8G
notes: When first starting the game, the main movie would crash the game. I tried kill (I.D), kill(I.D) again, system monitor and stop process, killall TheForest.exe(the game would not close) and eventually i gave up and  just reboot my pc. After rebooting and applying the launch option (  PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% ) the game ran just fine(for what it is). The gameplay was pretty awful, low to mid 20's at low/med setting's even though windows 10 at med/high would average around low/mid 50's. It is playable as in, you can move and do stuff but, most people would not like it.
title: The Forest
